ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.
text: Mes…õl…õ w…î, die me w…î me diema Yis…î asa anu, …îluak…õ mesulo k…õ …îbara yearahu y…õ muala, ngakula ne m…î nee b…õ nli m…î.
translated: When I was young, my mother used to tell me stories about Jesus Christ, and how he was born in a manger, and the angels sang and praised God, and the shepherds also went to see him.

ref: Jacob put servants in charge of each herd and told them, ‚ÄúGo ahead of me and keep a space between each herd.‚Äù
text: ∆Üvale …õhye m…î …îwulale ye sonvol…õma ne m…î asa anu. Ekpunli ko biala angome nee ye kavol…õ. Akee …îzele ye sonvol…õma ne m…î k…õ, ‚ÄúB…õze b…õlumua me nyunlu na b…õmaa adenle …õdeda b…õ ekpunli ekpunli ne m…î avinli.‚Äù
translated: The old man said to the children, "Let's go to the farm and work together. The farm is not a place for playing. When we get to the farm, I will tell you, 'Let's cultivate our land and plant our crops well, and we will have a good harvest.'""

ref: Hagar thought, ‚ÄúHave I really seen God and lived to tell about it?‚Äù So from then on she called him, ‚ÄúThe God Who Sees Me.‚Äù
text: Na Heega hanle k…õ, ‚ÄúAsoo amgba menwu Nyamenle anyunlu na met…õte aze?‚Äù Na …îv…õl…õle ∆êd…õnk…õma m…î…î nee ye dend…õle la, ‚ÄúNyamenle m…î…î nwu me la.‚Äù
translated: And Heega asked, "What is the name of the God who created the world?" And …îv…õl…õle ∆êd…õnk…õma replied, "The God who created me is the one."

ref: He treats us like foreigners and has even cheated us out of the bride price that should have been ours.
text: ∆Übu y…õ k…õ y…õle ny…õvol…õ, …îluak…õ yed…îne y…õ, y…õ…õ ezukoa biala m…î…î b…õk…õva b…õk…õmaa ye m…î…î le y…õ …õde…õ la …õnee yeva yeb…î …î nwo.
translated: The dog is very happy because it has seen its owner, it is wagging its tail and barking loudly to show its joy.

ref: Abraham separated seven female lambs from his flock of sheep,
text: Ebileham yele mboane mbel…õ nsuu vile ye mboane ne anu ziele ahane.
translated: The farmer went to the farm to work and returned with a basket of fish.

ref: But we will let you marry her, if you and the other men in your tribe agree to be circumcised.
text: Yem…îti saa b…õk…õy…õ k…õ y…õday…õ la, na b…õ mrenya ne m…î k…õy…õ b…õ mrenyanu nzonl…õ …õde…õ a, yem…î y…õk…õkponle zol…õ.
translated: When we go to the farm, we must take our cutlasses and hoes along, so that we can clear the weeds and cultivate the land properly.

ref: One day, Joseph told his brothers what he had dreamed, and they hated him even more.
text: Kenle ko Dwosefi hanle …õlal…õ, na m…î…î …îhanle …îhilele …î mediema ne m…î la, akee b…õhyile ye b…îk…î…î.
translated: When Dwosefi was going to the farm, he saw a big snake on the road, and he was very afraid, so he ran away quickly.

ref: Any man who isn't circumcised hasn't kept his part of the promise and cannot be one of my people.
text: Nrenya biala m…î…î any…õ ye mrenyanu nzonl…õ la …õtulu me ngyekyele…õ ne; …îw…î k…õ b…õye ye b…õfi me menli ne avinli.‚Äù
translated: The person who does not respect the elderly will not live long; if you do not respect them, they will not respect you either.

ref: Magdiel, and Iram. These clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom, the father of the Edomites. They took their names from the places where they settled.
text: Yis…î a zole Yid…îm mbusua ekpunli ekpunli …õhye m…î a: Temena, Aleva, Gyit…õte, ∆Üh…îlebama, Yila, Paen…în, Kinaeze, Timan, Mebezaa, Magede…õle, y…õ…õ Ayelam. ∆êleka m…î…î mbusua ekpunli ekpunli …õhye m…î ko biala d…õnlanle la, b…õvale abusua ekpunli ne duma b…õdonle ye.
translated: The Yid…îm family has many branches: Temena, Aleva, Gyit…õte, ∆Üh…îlebama, Yila, Paen…în, Kinaeze, Timan, Mebezaa, Magede…õle, and Ayelam. All these branches of the Yid…îm family have their own roots, but they all come from the same family tree.

ref: Although the man and his wife were both naked, they were not ashamed.
text: ∆ênee nrenya ne nee …î ye ne l…õ b…õ nwo z…î, noko b…õnze nwol…õ nyiane.
translated: The person who is not patient will not see the good things in life, because patience is a virtue.
